Mrs. May Arkwright Hutton, suf-
fraget leader, says there is no doubt
but her cause will be victorious
and she has canvassed the state,
and knows the situation thoroughly.


A canvass Of people on Riverside
avenue this morning resulted in
about half and half vote on the
question Of the state constitutional
amendment for equal suffrage. The
questions aroused a good deal of
amusement at some times and a
good study of human nature could
be made from the effect that the
question caused on the faces of the
ones Intercepted. On some, the
question caused a flicker of amuse-
ment.


"GUESS IT'S ALL RIGHT"


"Am l in favor of it? Well, I
guess it's all right. Wife's getting
pretty old now. anyway, so l guess
1t won't hurt her any, and If the
women want to vote. let 'em vote"
Some shook their heads with a
smile and passed on without saying
a word, while others paused to de-
liver lecture on the advantages or
disadvantages of the question.
Am l in favor of woman suf-


frage?" repeated one man. "Well.


| guess it's all right. I'm a good


deal like the man who said, If the


women want to suffer, let 'em suf-
| fer"


My wife runs things to suit her


self now, anyway, so guess it


won't hurt me any to have her
VOTE" said one philosophically.
Am l goin' to vote for woman
suffrage?" said one individual ag-
gressively. "See here, young fella,


you ought o. know better than that.


What do you think l am anyway ?"
and he passed on in disgust.
